description: Figure 8 presents the calculated segregation ratios (c_L/c_0) of Al and N as a function of solidification fraction (z_s) for three Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steel samples: 1# (x≈0.87 wt% Al), 2# (x≈2.77 wt% Al), and 3# (x≈4.70 wt% Al). The main panel displays six curves: solid lines denote Al segregation and dashed lines denote N segregation for each sample. An inset magnifies the region 0.90 ≤ z_s ≤ 1.00 to highlight the sharp upturn in segregation ratios near the end of solidification. The plot visually demonstrates that the degree of N segregation is markedly higher than that of Al across all samples. As z_s increases from 0 to 1.0, the solute enrichment in the residual liquid gradually rises and accelerates in the final stage. The citation notes that the combined segregation behavior of Al and N promotes AlN inclusion precipitation, and in the 3# sample, decreased N solubility delays the solidification fraction at which AlN forms. These thermodynamic calculation results align with the FactSage 8.2 solidification path analysis reported in the study.
